conflicts.description: Chapter 4 has Cliff correcting Mallick that Martin's study is upstairs on the left off the hall with a pocket door, but later chapters repeatedly place it downstairs past the half bath as the ground-floor office with the safe.
conflicts.evidence: Study said to be upstairs with a pocket door (snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:1148); then identified as two doors past the half bath (snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:1537) and as the study off the ground-floor hall just past the half bath (snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:3177).
conflicts.canonical_value: Martin's study is the downstairs office off the ground-floor hall, two doors past the half bath, with the safe behind the lake print.
conflicts.resolution_note: Kept the existing continuity-sheet value because the downstairs study location is repeated across later causally central scenes involving the safe, will, and RT envelope; chapter 4's upstairs/pocket-door wording reads as isolated manuscript inconsistency.

conflicts.description: The RT document's physical extent is inconsistent: chapter 8 says Cliff removes four legal-pad sheets and photographs eight images, chapter 16 says Joel opens two sheets, and chapter 17 refers to Cliff photographing fourteen pages.
conflicts.evidence: Cliff draws out four yellow legal-pad sheets (snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:2404) and photographs each sheet front and back for eight images (snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:2462); Joel later finds two sheets in the envelope (snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:5739); Paul later says Cliff photographed fourteen pages (snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:6150).
conflicts.canonical_value: RT document exists as Martin's handwritten August 17, 2002 account of the Ray Toomey fire; the updated sheet omits any exact sheet or photo count.
conflicts.resolution_note: The document's existence, date, authorship, and contents are stable and causally central, but the physical counts conflict across late-book references. To preserve canon without inventing a reconciliation, the sheet keeps only the stable facts.
